data.title: Morgan Stanley introduces stablecoin reserves fund for issuers to earn interest, complying with GENIUS Act rules.
data.description: Morgan Stanley has introduced a reserve fund for stablecoin that complies with regulations, enabling issuers to generate interest and adhere to the GENIUS Act. This initiative aims to provide a platform for issuers to earn returns while meeting regulatory requirements. The fund offers an avenue for stablecoin issuers to boost their earnings within the framework of the GENIUS Act.

data.title: Jane Street requests dismissal of Terraform insider trading lawsuit, claiming $40 billion collapse trades were based on public signals.
data.description: Asset management firm Jane Street is seeking the dismissal of an insider trading lawsuit filed by Terraform, citing that their trades were made based on publicly available signals. Terraform has accused Jane Street of insider trading during Terra's $40 billion collapse. Jane Street contends that their actions were legal and not motivated by any privileged information. The case highlights the complexities surrounding trading practices and legal obligations.

data.title: Tezos unveils sTEZ, a protocol-level liquid staking token to boost liquidity without relying on outside sources.
data.description: Tezos has recently unveiled sTEZ, a new liquid staking token created at the protocol level. This token is designed to address the balance between liquidity and network security within the Tezos ecosystem without relying on external sources. The introduction of sTEZ aims to enhance the overall efficiency and robustness of the Tezos network.

data.title: Trump criticizes prediction markets; White House warns against betting on decisions. His family invests in them, conflicting with his stance.
data.description: Staff members have been cautioned by the White House against engaging in bets on governmental choices. Nonetheless, the president's family ties to these companies contradict the message being conveyed, creating a conflict of interest.

data.title: Diana Shipping Inc. Calls on Genco Shipping & Trading to Stop Delaying 2026 Annual Meeting
data.description: Today, a preliminary proxy statement was filed that notably does not include the date of the annual meeting. This omission is despite reserving three different dates previously. The statement also does not specify the record date for shareholders. This development raises questions about the timeline and planning for the upcoming annual meeting.

data.title: LifeWave's "Code of Creation" Debuts at Sold-Out Beverly Hills Film Festival
data.description: In Draper, Utah, on April 24, 2026, LifeWave®, a prominent wellness technology company, showcased its documentary "Code of Creation" to a packed audience at the 26th Annual Beverly Hills Film Festival in Los Angeles on April 15, 2026. The event took place at the prestigious TCL Chinese Theater.

data.title: Soldier indicted for betting on US operation to capture President Nicolás Maduro of Venezuela, sparking controversy over prediction markets.
data.description: The soldier is facing charges for placing bets on the U.S. operation targeting President Nicolás Maduro of Venezuela. This incident has drawn attention to a novel form of gambling and potential cheating tactics.

data.title: These Analysts Increase Their Forecasts On Keurig Dr Pepper After Better-Than-Expected Q1 Results
data.description: Keurig Dr Pepper Inc. (NASDAQ: KDP) exceeded expectations with its first-quarter earnings report, revealing adjusted earnings per share of 39 cents, surpassing the projected 37 cents. The company, known for its ownership of popular brands like Dr Pepper, 7Up, Snapple, and Green Mountain Coffee, experienced a 9.4% year-over-year growth in quarterly sales, reaching $3.976 billion. This figure outperformed the expected $3.838 billion. Keurig Dr Pepper Inc. maintained its 2026 sales forecast at a range between $25.900 billion and $26.400 billion, slightly lower than the $26.081 billion predicted by analysts. The company expressed confidence in its strategic plans, execution quality, and cost management moving forward. More details can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Hospital Operator HCA Healthcare Flags Soft Respiratory Season As Volumes Lag
data.description: HCA Healthcare Inc., a major hospital operator in the U.S. (NYSE: HCA), recently reported its first-quarter 2026 revenues of $19.11 billion, showing a 4.3% year-over-year increase. This figure closely matched the consensus estimate of $19.10 billion. The company disclosed adjusted earnings of $7.15 for the quarter, up from $6.45 in the same period last year, aligning with market expectations. Within the same-facility metrics, there were varied volume trends: 0.9% growth in same-facility admissions and a 1.3% increase in equivalent admissions. On the other hand, same-facility emergency room visits rose by 0.3%, while inpatient surgeries and outpatient surgeries decreased by 0.3% and 1.7%, respectively. Additionally, there was a 3.1% growth in same-facility revenue per equivalent admission. HCA Healthcare's adjusted EBITDA reached $3.80 billion in the quarter, up from $3.73 billion compared to the previous year. More details can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Southwest Airlines Analysts Boost Their Forecasts Following Q1 Earnings
data.description: Southwest Airlines (NYSE: LUV) announced disappointing first-quarter financial results, revealing an adjusted earnings per share of 45 cents, falling short of the anticipated 47 cents. Revenue stood at $7.24 billion, below the projected $7.26 billion. CEO Bob Jordan acknowledged customer support for new products impacting financial performance. For the second quarter, the company forecasts adjusted earnings per share between 35 cents and 65 cents. The current Street estimate remains undisclosed. For further details, refer to Benzinga.com.
